member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified member types.
member.param: The types of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes
member.summary:
Specifies the types of members that are dynamically accessed.
This enumeration has a attribute that allows a bitwise combination of its member values.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.None
member.summary: Specifies no members.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicParameterlessConstructor
member.summary: Specifies the default, parameterless public constructor.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all non-public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all non-public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all non-public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all non-public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all non-public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all non-public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.Interfaces
member.summary: Specifies all interfaces implemented by the type.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructorsWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all non-public constructors, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicMethodsWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all non-public methods, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicFieldsWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all non-public fields, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicNestedTypesWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all non-public nested types, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicPropertiesWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all non-public properties, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicEventsWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all non-public events, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructorsWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all public constructors, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicNestedTypesWithInherited
member.summary: Specifies all public nested types, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.AllConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all constructors, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.AllMethods
member.summary: Specifies all methods, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.AllFields
member.summary: Specifies all fields, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.AllNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all nested types, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.AllProperties
member.summary: Specifies all properties, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.AllEvents
member.summary: Specifies all events, including those inherited from base classes.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All
member.summary: Specifies all members.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that an API is experimental and it may change in the future.
member.remarks: This attribute allows call sites to be flagged with a diagnostic that indicates that an experimental feature is used. Authors can use this attribute to ship preview features in their assemblies.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.param: The ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional message associated with the experimental attribute.
member.value: The message that provides additional information about the experimental feature.
member.remarks: This message can be used to provide more context or guidance about the experimental feature.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.
member.value: The format string that represents a URL to corresponding documentation.
member.remarks: An example format string is https://contoso.com/obsoletion-warnings/{0}.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.AllowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is allowed as an input even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DisallowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is disallowed as an input even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it. Specifies that an input argument was not null when the call returns.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter may be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the output will be non-null if the named parameter is non-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the associated parameter name.
member.param: The associated parameter name. The output will be non-null if the argument to the parameter specified is non-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the associated parameter name.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnAttribute
member.summary: Applied to a method that will never return under any circumstance.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method will not return if the associated Boolean parameter is passed the specified value.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified parameter value.
member.param: The condition parameter value. Code after the method will be considered unreachable by diagnostics if the argument to the associated parameter matches this value.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.ParameterValue
member.summary: Gets the condition parameter value.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with a field or property member.
member.param: The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the list of field and property members.
member.param: The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values when returning with the specified return value condition.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and a field or property member.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated field or property member will not be null.
The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and list of field and property members.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated field and property members will not be null.
The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the syntax used in a string.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.
member.param: The syntax identifier.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Initializes the with the identifier of the syntax used.
member.param: The syntax identifier.
Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Syntax
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the syntax used.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Arguments
member.summary: Optional arguments associated with the specific syntax employed.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.CompositeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing composite formats for string formatting.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.DateTimeFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing date and time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.EnumFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.GuidFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Json
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing JavaScript Object Notation (JSON).

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.NumericFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing numeric format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Regex
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing regular expressions.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeOnlyFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing time format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.TimeSpanFormat
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing format specifiers.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Uri
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing URIs.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.StringSyntaxAttribute.Xml
member.summary: The syntax identifier for strings containing XML.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: /// Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.IdHelpers
member.summary: Provides helper methods for monotonic ID generation.

member.name: M:System.IdHelpers.CreateMonotonicId(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
member.param: The timestamp to embed in the identifier.
member.returns: A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.

remarks.para: This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values: - High 64 bits: from the timestamp - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter

The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically, which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike Guid.CreateVersion7, which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.

member.name: T:System.ObsoleteAttribute
member.summary: Marks program elements that are no longer in use.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that compiler support for a particular feature is required for the location where this attribute is applied.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.FeatureName
member.summary: The name of the compiler feature.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.IsOptional
member.summary: If true, the compiler can choose to allow access to the location where this attribute is applied if it does not understand .

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RequiredMembers
member.summary: The used for the required members C# feature.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler
member.summary: Provides a handler used by the language compiler to process interpolated strings into instances.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.GuessedLengthPerHole
member.summary: Expected average length of formatted data used for an individual interpolation expression result.
member.remarks: This is inherited from string.Format, and could be changed based on further data. string.Format actually uses `format.Length + args.Length * 8`, but format.Length includes the format items themselves, e.g. "{0}", and since it's rare to have double-digit numbers of items, we bump the 8 up to 11 to account for the three extra characters in "{d}", since the compiler-provided base length won't include the equivalent character count.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.MinimumArrayPoolLength
member.summary: Minimum size array to rent from the pool.
member.remarks: Same as stack-allocation size used today by string.Format.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler._provider
member.summary: Optional provider to pass to IFormattable.ToString or ISpanFormattable.TryFormat calls.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler._arrayToReturnToPool
member.summary: Array rented from the array pool and used to back .

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler._chars
member.summary: The span to write into.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler._pos
member.summary: Position at which to write the next character.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler._hasCustomFormatter
member.summary: Whether provides an ICustomFormatter.
member.remarks: Custom formatters are very rare. We want to support them, but it's ok if we make them more expensive in order to make them as pay-for-play as possible. So, we avoid adding another reference type field to reduce the size of the handler and to reduce required zero'ing, by only storing whether the provider provides a formatter, rather than actually storing the formatter. This in turn means, if there is a formatter, we pay for the extra interface call on each AppendFormatted that needs it.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a handler used to translate an interpolated string into a .
member.param: The number of constant characters outside of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.
The number of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.
member.remarks: This is intended to be called only by compiler-generated code. Arguments are not validated as they'd otherwise be for members intended to be used directly.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Creates a handler used to translate an interpolated string into a .
member.param: The number of constant characters outside of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.
The number of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.
An object that supplies culture-specific formatting information.
member.remarks: This is intended to be called only by compiler-generated code. Arguments are not validated as they'd otherwise be for members intended to be used directly.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.IFormatProvider,System.Span{System.Char})
member.summary: Creates a handler used to translate an interpolated string into a .
member.param: The number of constant characters outside of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.
The number of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.
An object that supplies culture-specific formatting information.
A buffer temporarily transferred to the handler for use as part of its formatting. Contents may be overwritten.
member.remarks: This is intended to be called only by compiler-generated code. Arguments are not validated as they'd otherwise be for members intended to be used directly.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.GetDefaultLength(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Derives a default length with which to seed the handler.
member.param: The number of constant characters outside of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.
The number of interpolation expressions in the interpolated string.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.ToString
member.summary: Gets the built .

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.ToStringAndClear
member.summary: Gets the built and clears the handler.
member.remarks: This releases any resources used by the handler. The method should be invoked only once and as the last thing performed on the handler. Subsequent use is erroneous, ill-defined, and may destabilize the process, as may using any other copies of the handler after is called on any one of them.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.Clear
member.summary: Clears the handler.
member.remarks: This releases any resources used by the handler. The method should be invoked only once and as the last thing performed on the handler. Subsequent use is erroneous, ill-defined, and may destabilize the process, as may using any other copies of the handler after is called on any one of them.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.Text
member.summary: Gets a span of the characters appended to the handler.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendLiteral(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified string to the handler.
member.param: The string to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted``1(``0)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.
member.typeparam: The type of the value to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.
The format string.
member.typeparam: The type of the value to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted``1(``0,System.Int32)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.
Minimum number of characters that should be written for this value. If the value is negative, it indicates left-aligned and the required minimum is the absolute value.
member.typeparam: The type of the value to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted``1(``0,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.
The format string.
Minimum number of characters that should be written for this value. If the value is negative, it indicates left-aligned and the required minimum is the absolute value.
member.typeparam: The type of the value to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Writes the specified character span to the handler.
member.param: The span to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified string of chars to the handler.
member.param: The span to write.
Minimum number of characters that should be written for this value. If the value is negative, it indicates left-aligned and the required minimum is the absolute value.
The format string.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormattedSlow(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.
member.remarks: Slow path to handle a custom formatter, potentially null value, or a string that doesn't fit in the current buffer.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted(System.String,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.
Minimum number of characters that should be written for this value. If the value is negative, it indicates left-aligned and the required minimum is the absolute value.
The format string.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendFormatted(System.Object,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified value to the handler.
member.param: The value to write.
Minimum number of characters that should be written for this value. If the value is negative, it indicates left-aligned and the required minimum is the absolute value.
The format string.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.HasCustomFormatter(System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Gets whether the provider provides a custom formatter.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendCustomFormatter``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Formats the value using the custom formatter from the provider.
member.param: The value to write.
The format string.
member.typeparam: The type of the value to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.AppendOrInsertAlignmentIfNeeded(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Handles adding any padding required for aligning a formatted value in an interpolation expression.
member.param: The position at which the written value started.
Non-zero minimum number of characters that should be written for this value. If the value is negative, it indicates left-aligned and the required minimum is the absolute value.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.EnsureCapacityForAdditionalChars(System.Int32)
member.summary: Ensures has the capacity to store beyond .

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.GrowThenCopyString(System.String)
member.summary: Fallback for fast path in when there's not enough space in the destination.
member.param: The string to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.GrowThenCopySpan(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Fallback for for when not enough space exists in the current buffer.
member.param: The span to write.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.Grow(System.Int32)
member.summary: Grows to have the capacity to store at least beyond .

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.Grow
member.summary: Grows the size of .

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DefaultInterpolatedStringHandler.GrowCore(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Grow the size of to at least the specified .

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiredMemberAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a type has required members or that a member is required.

member.name: M:System.Text.EncodingExtensions.GetByteCount(System.Text.Encoding,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Gets the number of bytes required to encode the specified characters.

member.name: M:System.Text.EncodingExtensions.GetBytes(System.Text.Encoding,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Encodes the specified characters into the specified byte span.

member.name: T:System.Threading.ForceYielding
member.summary: await default(ForceYielding) to provide the same behavior as await Task.CompletedTask.ConfigureAwait(ConfigureAwaitOptions.ForceYielding).

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.DefaultMcpServerBuilder
member.summary: Default implementation of that enables fluent configuration of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. This builder is returned by the extension method and provides access to the service collection for registering additional MCP components.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.DefaultMcpServerBuilder.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The service collection to which MCP server services will be added. This collection is exposed through the property to allow additional configuration.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpMessageFilterBuilder
member.summary: Provides a builder for configuring message-level MCP server filters.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpMessageFilterBuilder.Services
member.summary: Gets the associated service collection.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpRequestFilterBuilder
member.summary: Provides a builder for configuring request-specific MCP server filters.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpRequestFilterBuilder.Services
member.summary: Gets the associated service collection.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder
member.summary: Provides a builder for configuring instances.

remarks.para: The interface provides a fluent API for configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers when using dependency injection. It exposes methods for registering tools, prompts, custom request handlers, and server transports, allowing for comprehensive server configuration through a chain of method calls.
The builder is obtained from the extension method and provides access to the underlying service collection via the property.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder.Services
member.summary: Gets the associated service collection.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpMessageFilterBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring message-level MCP server filters.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpMessageFilterBuilderExtensions.AddIncomingFilter(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpMessageFilterBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMessageFilter)
member.summary: Adds a filter to intercept all incoming JSON-RPC messages.
member.param: The message filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the message handler.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpMessageFilterBuilderExtensions.AddOutgoingFilter(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpMessageFilterBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMessageFilter)
member.summary: Adds a filter to intercept all outgoing JSON-RPC messages.
member.param: The message filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the message handler.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpRequestFilterBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring request-specific MCP server filters.
Adds a filter to the list resource templates handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the list tools handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the list prompts handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the get prompt handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the list resources handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the read resource handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the complete handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the subscribe-to-resources handler pipeline.
member.param: The request filter builder instance.
The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the unsubscribe-from-resources handler pipeline.

The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.summary: Adds a filter to the set logging level handler pipeline.

The filter function that wraps the handler.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides methods for configuring MCP servers via dependency injection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithTools``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The serializer options governing tool parameter marshalling.
member.remarks: This method discovers all instance and static methods (public and non-public) on the specified type, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each. For instance methods, an instance is constructed for each invocation of the tool.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithTools``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,``0,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The target instance from which the tools should be sourced.
The serializer options governing tool parameter marshalling.

remarks.para: This method discovers all methods (public and non-public) on the specified type, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each, using as the associated instance for instance methods.
However, if is itself an of , this method registers those tools directly without scanning for methods on .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithTools(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool})
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The instances to add to the server.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithTools(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Type},System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
Types with -attributed methods to add as tools to the server.
The serializer options governing tool parameter marshalling.
member.remarks: This method discovers all instance and static methods (public and non-public) on the specified types, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each. For instance methods, an instance is constructed for each invocation of the tool.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithToolsFromAssembly(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Reflection.Assembly,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds types marked with the attribute from the given assembly as tools to the server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The serializer options governing tool parameter marshalling.
The assembly to load the types from. If , the calling assembly is used.

remarks.para: This method scans the specified assembly (or the calling assembly if none is provided) for classes marked with the . It then discovers all methods within those classes that are marked with the and registers them as s in the 's .
The method automatically handles both static and instance methods. For instance methods, a new instance of the containing class is constructed for each invocation of the tool.
Tools registered through this method can be discovered by clients using the list_tools request and invoked using the call_tool request.
Note that this method performs reflection at runtime and might not work in Native AOT scenarios. For Native AOT compatibility, consider using the generic method instead.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithPrompts``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The serializer options governing prompt parameter marshalling.
member.remarks: This method discovers all instance and static methods (public and non-public) on the specified type, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each. For instance methods, an instance is constructed for each invocation of the prompt.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithPrompts``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,``0,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The target instance from which the prompts should be sourced.
The serializer options governing prompt parameter marshalling.

remarks.para: This method discovers all methods (public and non-public) on the specified type, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each, using as the associated instance for instance methods.
However, if is itself an of , this method registers those prompts directly without scanning for methods on .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithPrompts(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt})
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The instances to add to the server.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithPrompts(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Type},System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
Types with marked methods to add as prompts to the server.
The serializer options governing prompt parameter marshalling.
member.remarks: This method discovers all instance and static methods (public and non-public) on the specified types, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each. For instance methods, an instance is constructed for each invocation of the prompt.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithPromptsFromAssembly(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Reflection.Assembly,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)
member.summary: Adds types marked with the attribute from the given assembly as prompts to the server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The serializer options governing prompt parameter marshalling.
The assembly to load the types from. If , the calling assembly is used.

remarks.para: This method scans the specified assembly (or the calling assembly if none is provided) for classes marked with the . It then discovers all methods within those classes that are marked with the and registers them as s in the 's .
The method automatically handles both static and instance methods. For instance methods, a new instance of the containing class is constructed for each invocation of the prompt.
Prompts registered through this method can be discovered by clients using the list_prompts request and invoked using the prompts/get request.
Note that this method performs reflection at runtime and might not work in Native AOT scenarios. For Native AOT compatibility, consider using the generic method instead.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
member.remarks: This method discovers all instance and static methods (public and non-public) on the specified type, where the members are attributed as , and adds an instance for each. For instance members, an instance is constructed for each invocation of the resource.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources``1(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,``0)
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The target instance from which the resources should be sourced.

remarks.para: This method discovers all methods (public and non-public) on the specified type, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each, using as the associated instance for instance methods.
However, if is itself an of , this method registers those resources directly without scanning for methods on .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource})
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
The instances to add to the server.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Type})
member.summary: Adds instances to the service collection backing .
member.param: The builder instance.
Types with marked methods to add as resources to the server.
member.remarks: This method discovers all instance and static methods (public and non-public) on the specified types, where the methods are attributed as , and adds an instance for each. For instance methods, an instance is constructed for each invocation of the resource.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResourcesFromAssembly(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Reflection.Assembly)
member.summary: Adds types marked with the attribute from the given assembly as resources to the server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The assembly to load the types from. If , the calling assembly is used.

remarks.para: This method scans the specified assembly (or the calling assembly if none is provided) for classes marked with the . It then discovers all members within those classes that are marked with the and registers them as s in the 's .
The method automatically handles both static and instance members. For instance members, a new instance of the containing class is constructed for each invocation of the resource.
Resource templates registered through this method can be discovered by clients using the list_resourceTemplates request and invoked using the read_resource request.
Note that this method performs reflection at runtime and might not work in Native AOT scenarios. For Native AOT compatibility, consider using the generic method instead.

member.summary: Configures a handler for listing resource templates available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes resource template list requests.

remarks.para: This handler is responsible for providing clients with information about available resource templates that can be used to construct resource URIs.
Resource templates describe the structure of resource URIs that the server can handle. They include URI templates (according to RFC 6570) that clients can use to construct valid resource URIs.
This handler is typically paired with to provide a complete resource system where templates define the URI patterns and the read handler provides the actual content.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult})
member.summary: Configures a handler for listing tools available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler that processes list tools requests.

remarks.para: This handler is called when a client requests a list of available tools. It should return all tools that can be invoked through the server, including their names, descriptions, and parameter specifications. The handler can optionally support pagination via the cursor mechanism for large or dynamically generated tool collections.
When tools are also defined using collection, both sets of tools will be combined in the response to clients. This allows for a mix of programmatically defined tools and dynamically generated tools.
This method is typically paired with to provide a complete tools implementation, where advertises available tools and executes them when invoked by clients.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCallToolHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult})
member.summary: Configures a handler for calling tools available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes tool calls.
member.remarks: The call tool handler is responsible for executing custom tools and returning their results to clients. This method is typically paired with to provide a complete tools implementation, where advertises available tools and this handler executes them.

member.summary: Configures a handler for listing prompts available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler that processes list prompts requests.

remarks.para: This handler is called when a client requests a list of available prompts. It should return all prompts that can be invoked through the server, including their names, descriptions, and parameter specifications. The handler can optionally support pagination via the cursor mechanism for large or dynamically-generated prompt collections.

When prompts are also defined using collection, both sets of prompts will be combined in the response to clients. This allows for a mix of programmatically defined prompts and dynamically generated prompts.
This method is typically paired with to provide a complete prompts implementation, where advertises available prompts and produces them when invoked by clients.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithGetPromptHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult})
member.summary: Configures a handler for getting a prompt available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes prompt requests.

member.summary: Configures a handler for listing resources available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes resource list requests.

remarks.para: This handler is typically paired with to provide a complete resources implementation, where this handler advertises available resources and the read handler provides their content when requested.

member.summary: Configures a handler for reading a resource available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes resource read requests.
member.remarks: This handler is typically paired with to provide a complete resources implementation, where the list handler advertises available resources and the read handler provides their content when requested.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCompleteHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult})
member.summary: Configures a handler for auto-completion suggestions for prompt arguments or resource references available from the Model Context Protocol server.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes completion requests.
member.remarks: The completion handler is invoked when clients request suggestions for argument values. This enables auto-complete functionality for both prompt arguments and resource references.

member.summary: Configures a handler for resource subscription requests.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes resource subscription requests.

remarks.para: The subscribe handler is responsible for registering client interest in specific resources. When a resource changes, the server can notify all subscribed clients about the change.

This handler is typically paired with to provide a complete subscription management system. Resource subscriptions allow clients to maintain up-to-date information without needing to poll resources constantly.

After registering a subscription, it's the server's responsibility to track which client is subscribed to which resources and to send appropriate notifications through the connection when resources change.

member.summary: Configures a handler for resource unsubscription requests.
member.param: The builder instance.
The handler function that processes resource unsubscription requests.

remarks.para: The unsubscribe handler is responsible for removing client interest in specific resources. When a client no longer needs to receive notifications about resource changes, it can send an unsubscribe request.

This handler is typically paired with to provide a complete subscription management system. The unsubscribe operation is idempotent, meaning it can be called multiple times for the same resource without causing errors, even if there is no active subscription.
After removing a subscription, the server should stop sending notifications to the client about changes to the specified resource.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSetLoggingLevelHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SetLevelRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})
member.summary: Configures a handler for processing logging level change requests from clients.
member.param: The server builder instance.
The handler that processes requests to change the logging level.

remarks.para: When a client sends a logging/setLevel request, this handler will be invoked to process the requested level change. The server typically adjusts its internal logging level threshold and might begin sending log messages at or above the specified level to the client.
Regardless of whether a handler is provided, an should itself handle such notifications by updating its property to return the most recently set level.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithMessageFilters(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpMessageFilterBuilder})
member.summary: Configures message-level filters for the MCP server.
member.param: The builder instance.
A callback used to register message filters.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithRequestFilters(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.Action{Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpRequestFilterBuilder})
member.summary: Configures request-specific filters for the MCP server.
member.param: The builder instance.
A callback used to register request-specific filters.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithStdioServerTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder)
member.summary: Adds a server transport that uses standard input (stdin) and standard output (stdout) for communication.
member.param: The builder instance.

remarks.para: This method configures the server to communicate using the standard input and output streams, which is commonly used when the Model Context Protocol server is launched locally by a client process.

When using this transport, the server runs as a single-session service that exits when the stdin stream is closed. This makes it suitable for scenarios where the server should terminate when the parent process disconnects.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithStreamServerTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,System.IO.Stream,System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Adds a server transport that uses the specified input and output streams for communication.
member.param: The builder instance.
The input to use as standard input.
The output to use as standard output.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.CreateTarget(System.IServiceProvider,System.Type)
member.summary: Creates an instance of the target object.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for configuring MCP servers with dependency injection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions.AddMcpServer(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions})
member.summary: Adds the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to the service collection with default options.
member.param: The to add the server to.
An optional callback to configure the .
member.returns: An that can be used to further configure the MCP server.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.McpServerOptionsSetup
member.summary: Configures the McpServerOptions using additional services from DI.
member.param: The individually registered tools.
The individually registered prompts.
The individually registered resources.
The optional task store registered in DI.

member.summary: Configures the McpServerOptions using additional services from DI.
member.param: The individually registered tools.
The individually registered prompts.
The individually registered resources.
The optional task store registered in DI.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.McpServerOptionsSetup.Configure(ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions)
member.summary: Configures the given McpServerOptions instance by setting server information and collecting registered server primitives.
member.param: The options instance to be configured.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventIdFormatter
member.summary: Provides methods for formatting and parsing event IDs used by .
member.remarks: Event IDs are formatted as "{base64(sessionId)}:{base64(streamId)}:{sequence}".

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventIdFormatter.Format(System.String,System.String,System.Int64)
member.summary: Formats session ID, stream ID, and sequence number into an event ID string.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventIdFormatter.TryParse(System.String,System.String@,System.String@,System.Int64@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse an event ID into its component parts.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore
member.summary: An implementation backed by .

remarks.para: This implementation stores SSE events in a distributed cache, enabling resumability across multiple server instances. Event IDs are encoded with session, stream, and sequence information to allow efficient retrieval of events after a given point.
The writer maintains in-memory state for sequence number generation, as there is guaranteed to be only one writer per stream. Readers may be created from separate processes.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Options.IOptions{ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions},Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Configuration options for the store, including the to use.
Optional logger for diagnostic output.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore.CacheKeys
member.summary: Provides methods for generating cache keys.
member.remarks: Cache keys are versioned to allow format changes without conflicts with existing entries. When the cache format changes, increment to invalidate old entries.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore.CacheKeys.Version
member.summary: The current cache key version. Increment this when changing the cache format to ensure old entries are ignored.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore.StreamMetadata
member.summary: Metadata about a stream stored in the cache.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore.StoredEvent
member.summary: Serialized representation of an SSE event stored in the cache.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore.DistributedCacheEventStreamWriter.__LogEventAlreadyHasIdStruct
member.summary: This API supports the logging infrastructure and is not intended to be used directly from your code. It is subject to change in the future.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities
member.summary: Provides JSON serialization utilities for types.
member.remarks: This class provides source-generated serialization for the internal types used by to persist SSE events and stream metadata. It combines with to support the full object graph including and its derived types.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions
member.summary: Gets the instance for serializing distributed cache event stream store types.
member.remarks: This options instance combines the source-generated context for and with to support the full object graph including types.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.StreamMetadataJsonTypeInfo
member.summary: Gets the for .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.StoredEventJsonTypeInfo
member.summary: Gets the for .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.Boolean
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.JsonRpcMessage
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.StoredEvent
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.StreamMetadata
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.SseEventStreamMode
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.Int32
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.NullableInt32
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.Int64
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.String
member.summary: Defines the source generated JSON serialization contract metadata for a given type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.Default
member.summary: The default associated with a default instance.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreJsonUtilities.JsonContext.GeneratedSerializerOptions
member.summary: The source-generated options associated with this context.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.Cache
member.summary: Gets or sets the to use for event storage.
member.remarks: When using dependency injection with WithDistributedCacheEventStreamStore(), this is automatically populated from the registered in DI. Set this property explicitly to use a specific cache instance.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.EventSlidingExpiration
member.summary: Gets or sets the sliding expiration for individual events in the cache.
member.remarks: Events are refreshed on each access. If an event is not accessed within this time period, it may be evicted from the cache.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.EventAbsoluteExpiration
member.summary: Gets or sets the absolute expiration for individual events in the cache.
member.remarks: Events will be evicted from the cache after this time period, regardless of access.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.MetadataSlidingExpiration
member.summary: Gets or sets the sliding expiration for stream metadata in the cache.
member.remarks: Stream metadata includes mode and completion status. This should typically be set to a longer duration than event expiration to allow for resumability.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.MetadataAbsoluteExpiration
member.summary: Gets or sets the absolute expiration for stream metadata in the cache.
member.remarks: Stream metadata will be evicted from the cache after this time period, regardless of access.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.StreamReaderPollingInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the interval between polling attempts when a stream reader is waiting for new events in the default mode.
member.remarks: This only affects stream readers. A shorter interval provides lower latency for new events but increases cache access frequency.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.SingleSessionMcpServerHostedService
member.summary: Hosted service for a single-session (e.g. stdio) MCP server.
member.param: The server representing the session being hosted.
The host's application lifetime. If available, it will have termination requested when the session's run completes.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.SingleSessionMcpServerHostedService.#ctor(ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer,Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHostApplicationLifetime)
member.summary: Hosted service for a single-session (e.g. stdio) MCP server.
member.param: The server representing the session being hosted.
The host's application lifetime. If available, it will have termination requested when the session's run completes.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Throw
member.summary: Provides helper methods for throwing exceptions.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Obsoletions
member.summary: Defines diagnostic IDs, messages, and URLs for APIs annotated with .
member.remarks:
When a deprecated API is associated with a specification change, the message should refer to the specification version that introduces the change and the SEP when available. If there is a SEP associated with the experimental API, the Url should point to the SEP issue.
Obsolete diagnostic IDs are in the format MCP9###.
Diagnostic IDs cannot be reused when obsolete APIs are removed or restored. This ensures that users do not suppress warnings for new diagnostics with existing suppressions that might be left in place from prior uses of the same diagnostic ID.
